title: Phase 0 — Authoritative inventory and evidence
description: Non-blocking reporting milestone. The repository/package manifest is schema-valid; contested findings have evidence; release lineage and all audit scopes are recorded without destructive action.

title: Phase 1 — Source-of-truth policy and architecture decisions
description: Non-blocking reporting milestone. All blocking ADRs are accepted, published, and linked; no dependent implementation starts with an unresolved ownership or compatibility question.

title: Phase 2 — Security, compatibility, and release blockers
description: Non-blocking reporting milestone. Active disclosure and release-integrity risks are closed with regression tests and documented recovery paths.

title: Phase 3 — Build, workflow, and release consolidation
description: Non-blocking reporting milestone. Reusable automation is fixture-tested, immutable, fleet-realized, and continuously audited from one policy source.

title: Phase 4 — Foundation and cross-cutting contracts
description: Non-blocking reporting milestone. Canonical Result, time, serialization, validation, HTTP, and compatibility contracts are implemented with migration artifacts and API baselines.

title: Phase 5 — Messaging, data, web, and diagnostics hardening
description: Non-blocking reporting milestone. Runtime protocols have explicit semantics, bounded behavior, conformance/scenario coverage, and production-safe defaults.

title: Phase 6 — Migration, documentation, templates, and adoption
description: Non-blocking reporting milestone. Golden compositions pass the readiness gate; documentation is executable; templates are extracted only from proven compositions.

title: Phase 7 — Issue, PR, branch, and repository cleanup
description: Non-blocking reporting milestone. Audited work is migrated or completed, old artifacts link to replacements, and only verified branch/issue cleanup has occurred.

title: Phase 8 — Final fleet validation and closure
description: Non-blocking reporting milestone. Every managed repository is clean, documented, policy-conformant, CI-green, and release-ready, with evidence captured in Atya.Roadmap.
